steps.title: Use the intercept definition
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the $y$-axis. On the $y$-axis, $x=0$, so the correct point must start with $0$.

steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: At $x=0$, the curve passes through $y=2$. The intercept is therefore $(0,2)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C, $(2,0)$, is an $x$-intercept-style point, not a $y$-intercept. The graph's $y$-intercept is $(0,2)$, so B is correct.
